meta.name: TEQ Toronto Empathy Questionnaire
meta.description: The TEQ is a 16-item self-report scale that assesses an individual’s empathy. It measures the ability to understand and share the emotional states of others, serving as a concise and effective empathy measurement tool.
meta.author: Spreng, McKinnon, Mar & Levine (2009)
meta.reference: Spreng RN, McKinnon MC, Mar RA, Levine B. The Toronto Empathy Questionnaire: scale development and initial validation of a factor-analytic solution to multiple empathy measures. J Pers Assess. 2009;91(1):62-71.
meta.instruction: Please rate each statement based on your true feelings.

questions.text: When someone else is excited, I tend to get excited too
Other people’s misfortunes do not disturb me a great deal
It upsets me to see someone being treated disrespectfully
I am not really affected by other people’s positive emotions
I enjoy making other people feel better when they’re down
I am rarely moved by what happens to other people
I get angry when I see someone being taken advantage of
Sometimes I don’t feel very sorry for other people
When I see someone being hurt, I want to protect them
Other people’s feelings matter to me
When I see someone in an emergency, I feel helpless
I consider other people’s feelings before making decisions
When someone else cries, I feel like crying too
I don’t really care about other people’s needs
Seeing others treated unfairly doesn’t bother me much
I think showing compassion is a sign of weakness

ranges.label: Low Empathy
ranges.description: Your empathy score is significantly below average, suggesting difficulty understanding or sharing others' emotional experiences. Low empathy can affect relationship quality, social functioning, and workplace collaboration. Empathy is a skill that can be developed with practice.
suggestions: Practice active listening — focus on understanding others' feelings before responding
Read fiction or watch character-driven stories to practice perspective-taking
Engage in volunteer work to broaden your exposure to diverse experiences and viewpoints
Consider whether difficulty with empathy is affecting your relationships and seek professional guidance if needed

ranges.label: Below Average Empathy
ranges.description: Your empathy is below average, indicating some capacity to understand others' emotions but room for meaningful growth. You may find it easier to empathize in some situations than others. Developing empathic skills can strengthen your relationships and social connections.
suggestions: Practice asking open-ended questions about others' experiences and feelings
Try to imagine how situations look from the other person's perspective before reacting
Pay attention to nonverbal cues such as facial expressions and tone of voice
Reflect on times when you felt deeply understood and consider what made that possible

ranges.label: Above Average Empathy
ranges.description: Your empathy is above average, reflecting a strong ability to understand and share others' emotional experiences. This capacity supports healthy relationships, effective communication, and prosocial behavior. Continue nurturing this valuable interpersonal skill.
suggestions: Maintain your empathic engagement while setting healthy emotional boundaries
Use your empathy to support others during difficult times
Be mindful of compassion fatigue if you frequently absorb others' distress

ranges.label: High Empathy
ranges.description: Your empathy is very high, indicating exceptional sensitivity to others' emotional states. While highly empathic people build strong connections and are valued in helping roles, it is important to maintain boundaries to prevent emotional exhaustion or compassion fatigue.
suggestions: Practice self-care and emotional boundary-setting to protect against compassion fatigue
Channel your empathy into meaningful activities such as mentoring, counseling, or advocacy
Learn grounding techniques for moments when others' emotions feel overwhelming
